en.text: : for obedience: or, to the obedience of faith
I might have a prosperous journey
, that I may be comforted together
by the things that are made
: so: or, that they may be
; to retain: or, to acknowledge a reprobate: or, a mind void of judgment or, an unapproving mind
;) their conscience: or, the conscience witnessing with them the mean: or, between themselves
the things that are more excellent
; approvest: or, triest the things that differ
. guilty: or, subject to the judgment of God
; set forth: or, foreordained remission: or, passing over
. before him: or, like unto him
. in due time: or, according to the time
of him that was to come
.) by one man's: or, by one offence
. by the offence: or, by one offence by the righteousness: or, by one righteousness
? were: or, are were: or, are
in the likeness of his resurrection
. which: Gr. whereto ye were delivered
, even to him who is raised
. that being: or, being dead to that
? the body: or, this body of death
: for sin: or, by a sacrifice for sin
. to be carnally: Gr. the minding of the flesh to be spiritually: Gr. the minding of the Spirit
. the carnal: Gr. the minding of the flesh
. elder: or, greater elder: or, greater younger: or, lesser
? repliest: or, answerest again, or, disputest with God?
? our report: Gr. the hearing of us? report: or, preaching?
the olive tree which is wild
. concluded: or, shut them all up together
. giveth: or, imparteth with simplicity: or, liberally
; with: or, in the love of the brethren
. condescend: or, be contented with mean things
. not: or, not to judge his doubtful thoughts
. doubteth: or, discerneth and putteth a difference between meats damned: or, condemned, or liable to punishment
: according to: or, after the example of
in those things which pertain to
. much: or, many ways, or oftentimes
to be brought on my way
to him that is of power
